channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: June 11, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE President Trump said yesterday that Iran would “pay the price” for stalled peace negotiations. “We’ll…

item.title: 📍 British defense secretary John Healey resigns over defense funding | Breaking Defense
item.description: Healey said lack of sufficient funding in the forthcoming Defence Investment Plan could endanger UK security.

item.title: 📍 How Soccer Became ‘Un-American’ | Foreign Policy
item.description: The sport is a marker of a particular relationship to the wider world.

item.title: 📍 Mexico Could Be the World Cup’s Biggest Winner | Foreign Policy
item.description: As the United States closes itself off from the world, its co-host is doing the opposite.

item.title: 📍 FIFA’s Versailles Moment | Foreign Policy
item.description: Soccer is egalitarian. The 2026 FIFA World Cup is anything but.

item.title: 🏛️ The Hegseth Shock for New Zealand | Foreign Policy
item.description: Accusations of defense “freeloading” hit the third rail of Wellington’s politics.

item.title: 📍 PHOTOS: The sights of Berlin Air Show 2026 on Day 2 | Breaking Defense
item.description: A selection of photos from the second day of Germany’s largest aerospace expo.

item.title: 🕵️ Wargaming for Improved Acquisition: What Does It Take? | War on the Rocks
item.description: A few months ago, I attended a panel discussion for a wargame simulating rapid industrial mobilization for armed conflict. Conducted by a leading university, with teams composed of former senior defense officials, the game probed how government and industry collaboration would…

item.title: 🌏 The Chain of Peace: Do Supply Chain Chokepoints Deter War? | War on the Rocks
item.description: The next war over Taiwan may be deterred not by aircraft carriers or nuclear arsenals, but by a Dutch lithography machine. ASML, headquartered in Veldhoven, the Netherlands, is the sole manufacturer of the extreme ultraviolet lithography systems required to produce the world’s…

item.title: 🏛️ The Maritime Action Plan Needs a Yardstick: Enter the Mahan Ratio | War on the Rocks
item.description: Washington is littered with the corpses of grand plans to restore the Merchant Marine. The Trump administration’s Maritime Action Plan is the latest attempt, and to its credit, the most comprehensive since World War II.The plan is the government’s blueprint to resurrect…

item.title: 📍 Communicating geopolitics to young Australians is worth the effort, so let’s try | ASPI Strategist
item.description: A lack of meaningful context makes discussions of Australia’s foreign and national security policy too uninteresting or difficult for many young Australians to understand. This works against the long-term development of a public mandate for ...

item.title: 🕵️ Why UMNO is the Biggest Beneficiary of Malaysia’s PAS-Bersatu Divorce | The Diplomat
item.description: The fracturing of the opposition camp could give Malaysia's grand old party a springboard back to federal prominence.

item.title: 🌏 Why the Indo-Pacific Trade Hinges on East Africa | The Diplomat
item.description: The region's ports, sea lanes, and gas fields have become an important part of geopolitical competition, from the Gulf to the Pacific Ocean.

item.title: 📍 Philippine Foreign Secretary Says She Will Meet Myanmar Opposition Groups Soon | The Diplomat
item.description: The announcement comes as ASEAN member states move to normalize their relations with the new "civilian" government in Naypyidaw.

item.title: 🌏 The Real Problem With Global Trade | Foreign Affairs
item.description: How China’s currency manipulation is warping the world economy.

item.title: 📍 Silicon Valley’s Bad Bet on the Gulf | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Why the AI build-out was doomed from the start.

item.title: 🌏 Philippines Urges China to Remove ‘Movable Platform’ at Disputed South China Sea Shoal | The Diplomat
item.description: The country's coast guard said that the structure, which first appeared on satellite images on May 25, may be supporting unauthorized marine research.

item.title: 🕵️ A national-security asset, filed under ‘finance’ | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Once just a marketplace, the financial system is now a weapon of statecraft. Australia’s financial regulators hold the data that shows where the country is most exposed. That intelligence is a national-security asset but sits ...

item.title: 🌏 Xi Jinping’s Visit to Pyongyang: Regional Roundup | 38 North
item.description: Stimson experts weigh in on Chinese President Xi Jinping’s June 8-9 visit to North Korea ... The post Xi Jinping’s Visit to Pyongyang: Regional Roundup appeared first on 38 North.

item.title: 📍 Leading bipartisan lawmakers on how to achieve US energy ‘dominance’ | Atlantic Council
item.description: At the 2026 Global Energy Forum, lawmakers leading the charge on US energy security discussed their efforts and the role that allies can play. The post Leading bipartisan lawmakers on how to achieve US energy ‘dominance’ appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 Europe’s route out of this energy crisis, as mapped by Cyprus and Greece | Atlantic Council
item.description: At the 2026 Global Energy Forum, Greece's Stavros Papastavrou and Cyprus’s Michael Damianos outlined how the energy system is reshaping around the Eastern Mediterranean. The post Europe’s route out of this energy crisis, as mapped by Cyprus and Greece appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 📍 Somalia’s Fragile Government May Be on the Verge of Collapse | Foreign Policy
item.description: After disputes culminated in fighting last week, the country’s political system is in doubt.

item.title: 📍 Boeing’s New Larger Ghost Bat Can Carry AIM-120 AMRAAMs Internally | The War Zone
item.description: The stealthy Ghost Bat is heading into its third iteration, with larger wings, more power, and internal weapons bays. The post Boeing’s New Larger Ghost Bat Can Carry AIM-120 AMRAAMs Internally appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Trump Says More Strikes on Iran Are Coming | Foreign Policy
item.description: Tehran will “pay the price” for stalling negotiations, the U.S. president warned.

item.title: 📍 Increasing defense spending isn’t enough. The US and its allies must also guarantee interoperability. | Atlantic Council
item.description: Rising defense spending is creating a generational opportunity. But allied interoperability should be built into these new systems from the start. The post Increasing defense spending isn’t enough. The US and its allies must also guarantee interoperability. appeared first on…

item.title: 🏴 Mystery GPS outages traced to Russian satellite | Defense One
item.description: Occasional 10-second bursts of radio energy have knocked out antennae from Romania to Greenland.

item.title: 🏴 The future of NATO’s deterrence in the air domain | Atlantic Council
item.description: European NATO member states need to prepare to deter, and if necessary, defeat Russia—with less US support. When it comes to airpower, here are the actions European states need to take in the short, medium, and long term. The post The future of NATO’s deterrence in the air…

item.title: 🌍 Overnight Attacks Rattle U.S.-Iran Ceasefire (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: The latest round of tit-for-tat attacks between the U.S. and Iran are among the most severe since a ceasefire was reached on April 8. The post Overnight Attacks Rattle U.S.-Iran Ceasefire (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 CCA showdown and FCAS fallout, as Berlin Air Show gets underway | Breaking Defense
item.description: Catch what’s new on the first day of the sprawling defense expo outside the German capital.

item.title: 📍 Darwin Dialogue 2026: the conversation is over. It’s time for implementation | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Today marks the launch of the Darwin Dialogue 2026 report, From Exposure to Endurance: competitive endurance architecture for high-ESG mineral-specific supply chains. Australia and its partners have spent much of the past decade debating why ...
